File № 01 Origin Story Selma Delgado Archive

The Newsroom

Reagan High School  ·  Houston, Texas  ·  1988–1992

In ninth grade, I signed up for journalism because all the art classes were full. That unexpected detour introduced me to my first Macintosh computer and Adobe PageMaker — and everything changed. The newsroom had two kinds of computers: PCs for the writers, Macs for the designers. I lived on the Mac side, and design stopped being paper and paste-up boards. It became typography, branding, layout systems, and the early foundation of a career in digital craft.

Exhibit 01 · Origin

First Macintosh

Photograph · Reagan High School · Ninth grade

All the art classes were full. Journalism was the consolation. Then this beige box appeared on a desk — not for writing the stories (the PCs handled that) but for designing the paper itself. Typography. Layout. PageMaker. The rest of my career started compiling in the background.

01
02
Exhibit 02 · Credentials

Reagan Statesman Press Pass

1990 Spring Semester Permit · Sponsor: R. Bauer

"While on Official business — Before and After School, and During Lunch and Sixth Period Only." A laminated piece of cardstock that bought me license to roam the building with a camera and a question.

Exhibit 03 · Apprenticeship

Houston Chronicle, Floor 9

Temporary Badge · Editorial · Expires 6 July 1990

A month-long summer workshop for a handful of hand-picked students. Every morning at 8:00 sharp. Most students dropped out. I stayed the entire month, on the editorial floor, watching how a real newspaper got made.

03
04
Centerpiece
Exhibit 04 · Testimony

"Without Reservation"

Letter of Recommendation · 1 January 1991 · Robert Bauer, Publications Advisor

Two unprecedented years as editor-in-chief. A school paper expanded from four pages to eight. A principal who tried to censor a story. A student who refused. Robert Bauer documented all of it in a single page — and ended with a line I am still trying to live up to: "I could not have asked for more effort and concern in an editor."

Exhibit 05 · The Teacher

Robert Bauer

Photograph · Publications classroom · Holding his desk nameplate: "Journalism Sponsor"

The Publications Advisor. The man who ran the room with two kinds of computers: PCs along one wall for the reporters to write their stories, Macs along the other for the editor to design the paper. I lived on the Mac side. Every door this archive opens — every line of code, every layout, every campus I would later walk through — starts in this room, with this teacher.

05
06
Exhibit 06 · Farewell

"Honor This Permit as an Official Memory"

Ex-Editor-in-Chief Permit · Reagan Statesman · 1991

A handmade graduation gift from the newspaper staff, with the school bulldog and a request: "Please honor this permit as an official memory while I'm away at college." Three years of newsroom hours, traded in for a souvenir laminated by hand. I never threw it out.

Cover
Inside
Exhibit 07 · First Venture

University Press · Campus News Weekly

Fold-open business card · Self-designed · Houston, 1991–92

My first self-published design. Eighteen and just starting college, I tried to launch a campus newspaper — designed the logo, registered the P.O. box, ran the phone line, printed the card. The venture didn't take; nobody else came along to help build it. The artifact stayed. The first thing I ever designed for a client was a client of one — me.
